Fungal infection.description: Fungal infections affect skin, nails, or mucous membranes. Ayurveda classifies this as Kapha-Pitta imbalance.
Fungal infection.diet: Avoid sugar, dairy, and fermented foods. Eat neem, garlic, and turmeric regularly. Drink warm water with ginger.
Fungal infection.yoga: Pranayama (Kapalbhati, Anulom-Vilom) to boost immunity. Sun Salutation daily.
precautions: Keep affected area dry and clean

Allergy.description: Allergic reactions occur when the immune system overreacts to foreign substances. Ayurveda links this to Vata-Kapha imbalance.
Allergy.diet: Avoid cold, heavy, processed foods. Include warm soups, ginger tea, and honey.
Allergy.yoga: Anulom-Vilom Pranayama, Bhujangasana (Cobra Pose), Surya Namaskar.

GERD.description: GERD is caused by stomach acid flowing back into the esophagus. Ayurveda sees this as Pitta aggravation in the digestive tract.
GERD.diet: Avoid spicy, fried, acidic foods. Eat small frequent meals. Include coconut water, banana.
precautions: Don't lie down within 3 hours of eating

Chronic cholestasis.description: Reduced bile flow from liver. Ayurveda relates this to Pitta-Kapha imbalance affecting the liver (Yakrit).
Chronic cholestasis.diet: Low-fat diet, no alcohol. Include bitter greens, radish, and beets.

Drug Reaction.description: Unintended harmful response to a medication. Ayurveda attributes this to weak Agni and inability to process foreign substances.
Drug Reaction.diet: Light, easily digestible foods. Warm water. Avoid heavy, processed foods.
precautions: Carry a list of known drug allergies
Seek emergency care for severe reactions

Peptic ulcer diseae.description: Sores in the stomach lining or small intestine. Ayurveda describes this as Pittaja Grahani with vitiated Pitta.
Peptic ulcer diseae.diet: Avoid spicy, acidic, and fried foods. Eat soft cooling foods like rice, banana, and coconut milk.
Peptic ulcer diseae.yoga: Shavasana, Supta Baddha Konasana, avoid intense abdominal exercises.

AIDS.description: HIV/AIDS weakens the immune system over time. Ayurveda focuses on immune restoration (Rasayana therapy).
AIDS.diet: High-protein, nutrient-dense diet. Fresh fruits and vegetables. Avoid raw/undercooked foods.
AIDS.yoga: Gentle yoga and pranayama to strengthen immunity. Meditation for mental wellbeing.

Diabetes.description: Metabolic disorder with high blood sugar. Ayurveda calls this Madhumeha — a Kapha-dominant disorder with impaired metabolism.
Diabetes.diet: Low glycemic index foods. Avoid sugar, white rice, and refined carbs. Include barley, bitter gourd, and methi.
Diabetes.yoga: Mandukasana (Frog Pose), Paschimottanasana, Dhanurasana, Surya Namaskar.

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of stomach and intestines. Ayurveda treats this as Grahani or Atisar — Pitta-Vata aggravation.
Gastroenteritis.diet: Oral rehydration, clear fluids, rice gruel (kanji). Avoid dairy, spicy, and fatty foods until recovered.
Gastroenteritis.yoga: Rest primarily. Gentle abdominal massage with warm sesame oil.
precautions: Seek medical care if blood in stool

Bronchial Asthma.description: Chronic respiratory condition with airway inflammation. Ayurveda calls this Tamaka Shwasa — primarily Kapha-Vata disorder.
Bronchial Asthma.diet: Warm, light foods. Avoid cold beverages, dairy, heavy foods. Turmeric milk, ginger tea.
Bronchial Asthma.yoga: Pranayama (Anulom-Vilom, Bhramari, Kapalbhati), Matsyasana, Setu Bandhasana.

Hypertension.description: High blood pressure strains heart and blood vessels. Ayurveda attributes this to Vyana Vata and Pitta imbalance with Rakta vitiation.
Hypertension.diet: Low sodium, low fat. Include garlic, beetroot, pomegranate. Avoid alcohol.
Hypertension.yoga: Shavasana, Anulom-Vilom, Bhramari Pranayama, gentle Surya Namaskar.

Migraine.description: Intense recurring headaches often with nausea and light sensitivity. Ayurveda classifies this as Ardhavabhedaka — a Vata-Pitta disorder.
Migraine.diet: Avoid triggers: caffeine, alcohol, aged cheese, processed foods. Eat regular meals. Stay hydrated.
Migraine.yoga: Shavasana during attack. Between attacks: Pranayama, Yoga Nidra, Setu Bandhasana.
precautions: Avoid strong smells and bright lights during attack

Cervical spondylosis.description: Age-related wear on cervical vertebrae causing neck pain. Ayurveda calls this Greeva Stambha — primarily a Vata disorder.
Cervical spondylosis.diet: Anti-inflammatory diet. Avoid cold, dry, and raw foods. Include warm sesame oil, ghee, turmeric milk.
Cervical spondylosis.yoga: Gentle neck stretches, Bhujangasana, Shalabhasana. Avoid sudden neck movements.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).description: Brain hemorrhage-induced paralysis requires emergency care. Ayurveda's Pakshaghata therapy focuses on nerve rejuvenation.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).diet: Easy-to-digest, nutritious meals. High antioxidant foods. Avoid constipation.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).yoga: Physiotherapy-guided gentle movements. Pranayama for brain oxygenation under supervision.

Jaundice.description: Yellowing of skin and eyes due to elevated bilirubin. Ayurveda calls this Kamala — a Pitta-dominant condition affecting the liver.
Jaundice.diet: Light, easily digestible foods. Sugarcane juice, coconut water, papaya. No fatty or fried foods.
precautions: Seek immediate medical care for dark urine or high fever

Malaria.description: Parasitic infection transmitted by mosquitoes. Ayurveda addresses it as Vishama Jwara with Vata-Pitta aggravation.
Malaria.diet: Light, warm, easily digestible foods during fever. Avoid cold, heavy foods. Stay hydrated.
Malaria.yoga: Rest during acute phase. Gentle breathing exercises during recovery.
precautions: Complete the full course of treatment

Chicken pox.description: Highly contagious viral infection causing itchy blisters. Ayurveda treats this as Laghu Masurika affecting skin and immune system.
Chicken pox.diet: Light, easily digestible foods. Coconut water, fresh fruits. Avoid spicy, sour foods.
Chicken pox.yoga: Rest completely during active infection. Avoid spreading.
precautions: Stay isolated until all blisters crust over
Seek medical care for high fever or complications

Dengue.description: Mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever and joint pain. Ayurveda focuses on restoring Pitta balance and platelet support.
Dengue.diet: Papaya juice, coconut water, pomegranate juice. Light, nutrient-rich foods. Plenty of fluids.
Dengue.yoga: Complete rest during acute phase. No exercise.
precautions: Seek immediate hospital care if bleeding occurs

Typhoid.description: Bacterial infection causing prolonged fever and gastrointestinal symptoms. Ayurveda treats it as Pittaja Sannipataja Jwara.
Typhoid.diet: Soft, easily digestible foods: khichdi, rice gruel, boiled vegetables. Plenty of fluids.
Typhoid.yoga: Complete rest during fever. Breathing exercises during recovery.
precautions: Avoid raw foods from street vendors

Hepatitis A.description: Viral liver infection spread through contaminated food/water. Ayurveda focuses on Pitta-Kapha balance and liver rejuvenation.
Hepatitis A.diet: Low-fat, easily digestible foods. Avoid alcohol completely. Plenty of fluids. Fresh fruit juices.
Hepatitis A.yoga: Rest during acute phase. Gentle yoga after recovery.

Hepatitis B.description: Viral liver infection transmitted through blood and body fluids. Ayurveda focuses on liver support and immune strengthening.
Hepatitis B.diet: High-protein, low-fat diet. Avoid alcohol. Include green leafy vegetables and fresh fruits.
Hepatitis B.yoga: Gentle yoga and pranayama. Avoid strenuous exercise during active infection.

Hepatitis C.description: Chronic viral liver infection often without symptoms initially. Ayurveda aims at liver rejuvenation and detoxification.
Hepatitis C.diet: Anti-inflammatory diet. Low-fat foods. No alcohol. Include antioxidant-rich foods.
Hepatitis C.yoga: Gentle yoga, pranayama, and stress reduction techniques.
precautions: Do not share needles or personal hygiene items

Hepatitis D.description: Viral liver infection that only occurs alongside Hepatitis B. Ayurveda focuses on dual liver support and immune balance.
Hepatitis D.diet: Low-fat, high-protein diet. No alcohol. Fresh vegetables and fruits.
Hepatitis D.yoga: Gentle yoga and pranayama. Avoid strenuous activity.
precautions: Hepatitis B vaccination prevents Hepatitis D

Hepatitis E.description: Waterborne viral liver infection, usually self-limiting. Ayurveda focuses on detoxification and liver regeneration.
Hepatitis E.diet: Light, easily digestible foods. No alcohol. Plenty of fluids.
Hepatitis E.yoga: Rest during acute phase. Gentle breathing exercises during recovery.

Alcoholic hepatitis.description: Liver inflammation caused by heavy alcohol consumption. Ayurveda focuses on liver detoxification and complete alcohol cessation.
Alcoholic hepatitis.diet: High-protein, vitamin-rich foods. No alcohol whatsoever. Small, frequent meals.
Alcoholic hepatitis.yoga: Gentle yoga after acute phase subsides. Stress management through meditation.

Tuberculosis.description: Bacterial infection primarily affecting the lungs. Ayurveda treats TB (Rajayakshma) with Rasayana (rejuvenation) therapy.
Tuberculosis.diet: High-calorie, high-protein diet. Include milk, eggs, nuts. Avoid smoking completely.
Tuberculosis.yoga: Pranayama (gentle), Bhujangasana, Shalabhasana. Fresh air and sunlight.
precautions: Complete the full TB medication course (6+ months)

Common Cold.description: Viral upper respiratory infection. Ayurveda treats this as Pratishyaya — typically a Vata-Kapha imbalance.
Common Cold.diet: Warm soups, herbal teas, ginger honey lemon. Avoid cold foods and beverages.
Common Cold.yoga: Steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil. Gentle Anulom-Vilom. Jala Neti (nasal rinse).

Pneumonia.description: Lung infection causing inflammation in air sacs. Ayurveda treats this as Shwasroga with Pitta-Kapha aggravation.
Pneumonia.diet: Warm, light, easily digestible foods. Warm water, herbal teas. No cold foods.
Pneumonia.yoga: Gentle breathing exercises during recovery. Pranayama after acute phase.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).description: Swollen veins in the rectum or anus. Ayurveda calls this Arsha — primarily a Vata-Pitta disorder.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).diet: High-fiber diet: fruits, vegetables, whole grains. Plenty of water. Avoid spicy, fried foods.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).yoga: Ashwini Mudra, Pawanmuktasana, Malasana (squat). Avoid straining.

Heart attack.description: Blocked blood supply to heart muscle. Ayurveda focuses on Hridaya (heart) strengthening and Vata-Pitta balance.
Heart attack.diet: Heart-healthy diet: low fat, low sodium, high fiber. Mediterranean diet principles. No alcohol or tobacco.
Heart attack.yoga: Light Pranayama after recovery. Walking. No strenuous exercise without medical clearance.

Varicose veins.description: Enlarged, twisted veins usually in legs. Ayurveda relates this to Vata-Rakta imbalance affecting circulation.
Varicose veins.diet: Anti-inflammatory foods. High-fiber diet. Stay hydrated. Avoid salt.
Varicose veins.yoga: Legs up the wall (Viparita Karani), leg lifts, ankle rotations. Avoid prolonged standing.

Hypothyroidism.description: Underactive thyroid gland producing insufficient hormones. Ayurveda relates this to Kapha aggravation and Agni (metabolic fire) reduction.
Hypothyroidism.diet: Iodine-rich foods (seaweed, iodized salt). Avoid goitrogens in large quantities. Include selenium-rich foods.
Hypothyroidism.yoga: Sarvangasana (shoulder stand), Halasana, Matsyasana. Regular exercise to boost metabolism.
precautions: Avoid calcium supplements near thyroid medication

Hyperthyroidism.description: Overactive thyroid producing excess hormones. Ayurveda treats this as Pitta aggravation with Vata vitiation.
Hyperthyroidism.diet: Calcium-rich foods. Avoid iodine-rich foods. Include goitrogen-containing foods (broccoli, cabbage). No caffeine.
Hyperthyroidism.yoga: Cooling pranayama (Sheetali, Sheetkari), Shavasana. Avoid overheating.
precautions: Protect eyes if Graves disease present

Hypoglycemia.description: Abnormally low blood sugar levels. Ayurveda sees this as Vata aggravation affecting glucose metabolism.
Hypoglycemia.diet: Regular small meals every 2-3 hours. Include complex carbs, protein. Avoid refined sugar, alcohol.
Hypoglycemia.yoga: Gentle yoga. Avoid intense exercise without eating. Bhramari Pranayama for stress.

Osteoarthristis.description: Degenerative joint disease causing cartilage breakdown. Ayurveda calls this Sandhivata — a Vata disorder affecting joints.
Osteoarthristis.diet: Anti-inflammatory diet. Omega-3 rich foods. Avoid nightshades. Include turmeric, ginger.
Osteoarthristis.yoga: Swimming (low impact), gentle stretches, Trikonasana. Avoid high-impact activities.
precautions: Hot or cold compress for pain

Arthritis.description: Inflammation of joints causing pain and stiffness. Ayurveda treats as Amavata — Ama toxins + Vata aggravation.
Arthritis.diet: Anti-inflammatory: turmeric, ginger, omega-3s. Avoid processed foods, red meat, sugar.
Arthritis.yoga: Gentle joint movements, Surya Namaskar (modified), Pranayama.

(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.description: Brief episodes of dizziness from head position changes. Ayurveda relates this to Vata imbalance affecting the nervous system.
(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.diet: Reduce salt and caffeine. Stay hydrated. Avoid alcohol.
(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.yoga: Balasana (Child's Pose), gentle neck rolls. Avoid sudden head movements.

Acne.description: Inflammatory skin condition affecting oil glands and hair follicles. Ayurveda treats acne (Yuvanpidika) as a Pitta-Kapha imbalance.
Acne.diet: Avoid dairy, sugar, and high glycemic foods. Include antioxidant-rich vegetables, fruits.
Acne.yoga: Pranayama (Kapalbhati), Sarvangasana. Regular exercise to reduce stress.

Urinary tract infection.description: Bacterial infection in the urinary system. Ayurveda calls this Mutraghata or Mutrakriccha — Pitta and Kapha vitiation.
Urinary tract infection.diet: Plenty of water. Cranberry juice. Avoid spicy, acidic foods. Include cooling foods.

Psoriasis.description: Chronic autoimmune skin condition causing rapid cell turnover. Ayurveda treats this as Kitibha Kushtha — Vata-Kapha skin disorder.
Psoriasis.diet: Anti-inflammatory diet. Avoid alcohol, red meat, processed foods. Include omega-3 rich foods.
Psoriasis.yoga: Stress reduction: Yoga Nidra, Pranayama, Shavasana. Stress is a major trigger.

Impetigo.description: Highly contagious bacterial skin infection common in children. Ayurveda treats skin infections (Kushtha) with blood-purifying herbs.
Impetigo.diet: Avoid sugar and junk food. Include vitamin C and zinc-rich foods for healing.
Impetigo.yoga: No specific yoga during active infection. Focus on hygiene.
precautions: Keep infected area clean and covered
Wash hands frequently to prevent spreading
